draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to approachable, friendly UI shapes that feel less corporate and more human
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace curved cards, buttons, and containers; soften the overall product personality
facade.rejectImplies: Consider cleaner, more geometric or angular design language; sharper edges may feel more professional and efficient

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to approachable, friendly UI shapes that feel less corporate and more human
evidence.implication: Embrace curved cards, buttons, and containers; soften the overall product personality

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer snappy, zero-lag contact interactions over smooth animated entries
facade.label: Contacts pop in instantly
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should minimize motion—clicks land immediately, lists load snap-to, no easing
facade.rejectImplies: The CRM should favor fluid transitions—contacts slide/fade in, interactions feel responsive through motion, not speed

facade.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers works best when the interface strips away visual noise—showing only essential client info and next actions—creating calm focus rather than overwhelming detail.
facade.label: Minimal dashboard
facade.acceptImplies: User values clarity and ease of navigation; likely prefers a stripped-back aesthetic that feels purposeful rather than feature-heavy.
facade.rejectImplies: User may want richness, personality, or more visual feedback; they might prefer a warmer, more visually generous interface that feels less austere.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need persistent access to nav while scanning clients/projects—a fixed left sidebar paired with card-based content areas optimizes both navigation and quick visual scanning
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore denser layouts where nav stays visible; test card-based project/client listing; consider collapsible sidebar for mobile
facade.rejectImplies: Explore full-width tab systems or top nav; test list-based layouts instead of cards; consider content-first designs that hide navigation

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer scannable contact cards over dense table rows for quick visual lookup of client info
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data vs key metrics — do CRM views prioritize comprehensive data display or at-a-glance summaries?
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick visual scanning and prefers modular, spacious layouts over information density; card-based UI likely feels more approachable
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers efficient data density and table formats; may want to see multiple clients and fields simultaneously without scrolling; values spreadsheet-like familiarity

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to friendly, approachable language than corporate formality—they want a tool that feels like it's made *for them*, not *at them*.
facade.label: Casual, conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into warmth, humor, and direct address. Use 'you' and 'we'. Short sentences. Contractions. Maybe even an occasional emoji or playful microcopy.
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward polished, formal voice. Third-person or passive constructions. Industry jargon. Neutral, buttoned-up tone throughout.

axes.label: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels more dynamic and branded
Whether they favor sidebar/list-based navigation vs. card/grid layouts—reveals how they want to organize and access client/project information at a glance
Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business tool that feels corporate or one that feels like a supportive partner
Whether they prefer calm, minimal atmosphere vs. energetic, motivating atmosphere—reveals if they want a tool that feels like quiet focus or one that feels like momentum and progress

Whether they want dense information displays (all stats visible) vs. progressive disclosure (show essentials, drill down for details)—reveals how they prefer to manage complexity in their freelance business
Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, animated transitions—reveals if they value speed and directness or prefer a more polished, guided experience

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels more dynamic and branded
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they favor sidebar/list-based navigation vs. card/grid layouts—reveals how they want to organize and access client/project information at a glance
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business tool that feels corporate or one that feels like a supportive partner
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer calm, minimal atmosphere vs. energetic, motivating atmosphere—reveals if they want a tool that feels like quiet focus or one that feels like momentum and progress
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want dense information displays (all stats visible) vs. progressive disclosure (show essentials, drill down for details)—reveals how they prefer to manage complexity in their freelance business
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, animated transitions—reveals if they value speed and directness or prefer a more polished, guided experience
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers working across diverse client projects may prefer a neutral monochrome interface that doesn't compete with their own creative work, or conversely, a colorful UI that energizes and motivates throughout the workday
facade.label: Monochrome or colorful
facade.acceptImplies: Pursue a restrained, grayscale-dominant palette with accent colors only for critical actions
facade.rejectImplies: Explore a multi-color system with distinct hues for different sections, projects, or status types

draft.summary: A lightweight CRM dashboard for freelancers to track projects, clients, and invoices in one place.
